Product Design & Research RentSpree · 2025
Jump to Challenge My Role Research Strategy The MVP Launch Reflection

Landlord Banking, Rent Collection, and the Revenue Case for Financial Tools

RentSpree was built on tenant screening. Expanding into landlord banking, automated rent collection, and financial management represented a fundamentally new revenue model. I led design and research for an intentionally lean MVP to validate whether landlords would trust RentSpree with their money before we invested in scale.

MVP Strategy Product Design User Research Banking & Financial Tools Fintech UX Cross-functional Leadership Team Resilience
3 mo Kickoff to launch
65% of PRO subscribers with active payment schedules
4 Core financial features
Nov 4 Launch date, 2025

The Challenge

A trusted screening platform with an untapped financial tools opportunity

RentSpree had proven strong product-market fit in tenant screening and rent collection. But our research uncovered a larger opportunity: landlords were struggling to manage their financial lives. Most relied on spreadsheets, disconnected bank accounts, and manual bookkeeping to track income, expenses, cash flow, and tax readiness. This fragmentation created friction, missed insights, and wasted time during peak seasons.

📊

Financial Fragmentation

Rent income, expenses, and vendor payments scattered across spreadsheets, bank apps, and email records.

⏱️

Manual Bookkeeping Burden

DIY landlords spend hours each month reconciling transactions, organizing receipts, and preparing tax documents.

🔍

Lack of Clarity

Without a consolidated view, landlords struggle to understand real-time rental performance or profitability.

🚫

Trust Barriers to Fintech

Landlords are price-sensitive and skeptical of new financial tools. They need clear proof of value before switching systems.

MVP Overview

How the MVP Came Together

A consolidated view of the MVP scope, feature prioritisation, and the decisions that shaped what shipped.

Design Artifacts

Selected screens and deliverables from the project.

My Role

Head of Product Design and Research

I led the product design and research strategy for the landlord subscription MVP, working across a cross-functional squad to balance speed with intentionality. My decisions shaped three key areas: the research approach to validate messaging before launch, the design philosophy that prioritized pragmatic clarity over polish, and the leadership approach to maintain momentum through unexpected team disruption.

In September 2025, our squad lost its engineering manager unexpectedly. Rather than pause, I worked with leadership to quickly backfill the role and kept the team aligned on our shipping timeline. This required clear communication about priorities, a ruthless focus on MVP scope, and trust in the team's ownership of the work.

Key Decisions
🎯
Research First
Run a messaging concept test before launch to uncover how landlords interpret financial tool positioning, so GTM messaging is built on user insights, not assumptions.
⚡
Intentionally Scrappy
Ship with core features only, minimal polish. Observe real usage patterns and organic adoption before investing in refinement. Speed to market validates the opportunity faster.
🤝
Clarity Over Tone
Landlords prioritize operational details and proof points over aspirational language. Every value claim must be paired with a concrete "how it works" line.

Research & Discovery

Messaging concept test revealed what landlords actually care about

Before launch, I led a research initiative to test three distinct messaging approaches for the landlord subscription offering. Six landlords with 1-3 rental units reviewed concepts focused on Control (confidence and peace of mind), Simplicity (operational efficiency), and Growth (investment and scaling). The findings were clear and actionable.

🎯
Key Finding: Rent Collection Automation Was the Most Compelling Feature

Every concept performed well, but the single most appealing element across all three was automated rent collection. Landlords said things like "professional rent collection with no chasing" and "no surprises." This became our north star for positioning and feature prioritization.

How Each Messaging Approach Landed

🛡️
Control
"Peace of mind and professionalism"

Appealed to landlords' desire for confidence and tax-time readiness, but felt high-level unless paired with operational detail. Some participants found parts "fluffy."

Strength
Emotional resonance
✓
Weakness
Requires re-reads
⚠️
✨
Simplicity
"One clear financial picture"

Produced the strongest speed-to-comprehension advantage. Direct language and plain framing made the benefit immediately clear: consolidated rent, expenses, and cash flow in one place.

Strength
Clarity and speed
✓
Winner
Most recommended
⭐
📈
Growth
"Support your business goals"

Strongly appealed to investor-minded landlords thinking about scaling. Felt like professionalization. But amplified requests for banking specifics (which bank, fees, card mechanics).

Strength
Investment mindset
✓
Segment fit
Portfolio-growth focused
⚠️

"Landlords prioritize pragmatic clarity over tone: every benefit statement must be anchored immediately to a concrete, verifiable mechanism. Aspirational language without an adjacent 'how it works' line leads users to pause and ask operational questions."

Research Finding

Critical Gaps Across All Concepts

Testing revealed consistent questions landlords asked, regardless of messaging approach. This informed our go-to-market strategy and product documentation:

Banking Partner & Custody
Which bank backs the account? Where do funds sit? Do they sweep to my personal bank or stay in RentSpree?
Card Type & Rewards
Is it credit or debit? Do I earn points? How do rewards apply to contractor/vendor spend?
Integration with Existing Tools
How do Zelle, Venmo, and cash transactions get recorded? Does this work with QuickBooks?
Pricing & Trial Duration
What does it cost? How long is the free trial? Landlords need a full cycle to evaluate fairly.

Design Strategy

The MVP-first, learn-fast philosophy

Rather than perfect the product before launch, I advocated for an intentionally scrappy MVP approach. This required discipline around scope, trust in the team, and a willingness to let real users guide iteration. The strategy had three pillars.

Speed to Market + Organic Adoption + Rapid Iteration = Market Validation
The MVP formula: Ship core features quickly, observe real behavior, make data-informed improvements.
  • 1

    Ruthless Scope Definition

    We shipped four core features: expense management, banking integration, performance dashboard, and rent collection automation. Anything aspirational (tax documents, vendor integrations, advanced reporting) was deferred. This clarity kept the team focused and the timeline realistic.

  • 2

    Design for Learning, Not Perfection

    User interface was functional and clean, not polished. Copy prioritized clarity over brand voice. We built in monitoring for drop-off points, conversion friction, and feature adoption so we could identify what to improve next.

  • 3

    Lead Through Disruption

    When the engineering manager departed in September, the team could have lost momentum. Instead, I kept the squad aligned on the October design freeze and November go-live. We hired a replacement EM who came in with fresh energy and helped push through final sprints.

The MVP Launch

Landlord PRO went live November 4, 2025

The MVP shipped with four integrated financial tools designed to address the core landlord pain point: fragmented financial management. The positioning drew from research insights, with clarity and operational proof at the forefront.

Core Features

💰
Expense Management
Track rental income and expenses in one place. Automatically categorize transactions and generate performance snapshots.
🏦
Banking Integration
Access a dedicated rental account with high-yield features and a business card designed for landlord expense spend.
📊
Performance Dashboard
Instant clarity on rental performance, cash flow, and profitability across all properties in one view.
⚙️
Rent Collection Automation
Tenants pay automatically. Late fees apply automatically. Documentation is timestamped and stored for disputes and taxes.

"With Landlord PRO, we are giving landlords the modern financial management tools they have been waiting for, all within the RentSpree experience they already trust."

Launch Announcement

Launch Positioning

The GTM messaging leaned heavily on the research insights: we led with simplicity and operational clarity. Every benefit claim was paired with a concrete proof point. Banking details (partner credibility, fund custody, card mechanics) were front and center to address trust barriers immediately.

We avoided aspirational language like "go from guesswork to growth" unless it was immediately followed by the operational mechanism that makes it true. This pragmatic approach resonated with the DIY landlord mindset the research revealed.

Launch & Early Signals

Observing organic adoption and validating the opportunity

The MVP launched with organic adoption as the key metric. We intentionally did not over-invest in paid acquisition or complex GTM mechanics. Instead, we monitored how existing landlords discovered, adopted, and engaged with the product, using real behavior to guide the next phase of iteration.

Tenant Payment Adoption
Percentage of landlords enabling rent collection automation (Nov-Feb 2026)
Feature Usage Frequency
Monthly active usage by feature type

What Validated

Rent automation truly resonates
The feature we identified in research as most compelling is driving the highest engagement. Landlords are activating tenant autopay faster than we anticipated.
Financial transparency is a win
The consolidated dashboard is becoming a regular touchpoint. Landlords are returning weekly to check cash flow and performance metrics.
Organic word-of-mouth is real
Without paid campaigns, landlords are discovering the product through existing RentSpree touchpoints and within-app prompts.
Trust barriers are lower than expected
With clear banking details and integration transparency upfront, adoption concerns were fewer than research suggested.

What Still Needs Work

Conversion rate is scrappy
We're converting a smaller percentage of invited landlords than we'd like. This requires refinement to the invite timing, messaging, and value articulation in-product.
Expense categorization needs help
Users struggle to categorize some transaction types. The UI requires iteration to make categorization faster and more intuitive.
Banking features need more guidance
While trust barriers are lower, landlords want more documentation about card rewards, account features, and how bank relationships work in the broader RentSpree ecosystem.
Onboarding is too complex
The setup flow requires multiple steps. We're seeing drop-off before subscription activation. This is the highest-priority improvement for Q1 2026.

Reflection & Next Steps

Honest assessment of what worked and what needs to evolve

This MVP was a deliberate trade-off. We shipped faster than our historical run rate by staying ruthless about scope and accepting intentional roughness. The payoff was clear: we got into market, validated the landlord financial tools opportunity, and built a foundation for rapid iteration based on real user behavior rather than assumptions.

By Design, Scrappy in the Right Ways

The scrappiness was intentional. Simple UI, minimal polish, direct copy, lean feature set. This allowed the team to move fast and forced us to prioritize ruthlessly. The trade-off was that some UX friction exists that would be easy to fix with one more design iteration, but the speed-to-learning outweighed that polish cost at this stage.

What I'd Do Differently

  • 1

    Invest more in onboarding discovery upfront

    We could have done a round of usability testing on the signup flow before launch. That would have surfaced the drop-off friction early and reduced rework post-launch.

  • 2

    Build more comprehensive in-product guidance

    Landlords have questions about card mechanics, banking details, and integration behavior that should be surfaced in-product, not buried in help docs. Future iterations should embed more contextual help and progressive disclosure.

  • 3

    Establish clearer success metrics earlier

    We knew we wanted to observe organic adoption, but we could have been more specific about thresholds and decision gates upfront. This would have sharpened our post-launch analysis and made prioritization clearer.

    • The Path Forward

    Landlord PRO has proven the financial tools opportunity. Landlords want this. They're using it. What's next is refinement: improving onboarding, deepening feature polish, expanding the product roadmap based on real usage data. The MVP fulfilled its purpose as a validation vehicle. Now the work shifts to scaling with intention.
